SEARCH

All Classes and Interfaces

Class
Description
AbstractHandler
Base implementation of the Handler interface and extends UnicastRemoteObject.
AnsiColor
 
Card
The abstract Card class represents a card of the game.
ChatMessage
The ChatMessage class represents a message.
ChooseColorController
The controller of the scene where you have to choose the color
ChooseConnectionController
The controller of the scene where you have to put the server's IP and choose the type of connection
ChooseObjectiveController
The controller of the scene where the objective card is chosen
ChooseStarterController
The controller of the scene where the starter card is chosen
Cli
The Cli class extends View and implements Runnable.
Client
Abstract class representing a client in the network.
ClientApp
 
ClientToServerMessage
Represents a message sent from the client to the server.
ColorAvailableMessage
Message from server to client notifying the available player colors.
ColorChosenMessage
Message from server to client notifying that a player has chosen a color.
Content
This enum class represents every resource and object of the game.
ContentCounter
This class represents a counter to keep track of a player's number of resources and objects.
Controller
The Controller interface serves as a common static type for all controllers.
Coordinate
This class represents a point in two-dimensional space.
CoordinatesAvailableMessage
Message from server to client notifying available coordinates for placing cards.
CreateGameController
The controller of the scene where a new game is created
CreateGameMessage
Message from client to server to request a new game creation.
CurrentPlayerHasPlayedMessage
Message from server to client indicating whether the current player has already played a card in the current turn.
CurrentPlayerMessage
Message from server to client indicating the current player.
Deck
Represents a deck of cards used in a game.
DeckEmptyException
Exception thrown when draw is attempted on an empty deck.
DisplayedObjectiveMessage
Message from server to client containing the displayed objective cards.
DisplayedPlayableCardsMessage
Message from server to client containing the displayed playable cards.
DrawMessage
Message from client to server to request to draw a card
ExceptionMessage
Message from server to client to notify an exception occurred on the server.
Face
The abstract Face class represents a face of a card.
FirstMessage
Message sent from server after the connection is established to set clientID.
Game
Class Game Description: This class manages the state and behavior of the game.
GameBoardController
Controller to manage the game GUI.
GameController
The GameController class implements the Controller interface to manage game-related logic and interactions.
GameJoinedMessage
Message sent from server to client when the client successfully joins a game.
GameManager
Class GameManager Description: This class manages the lifecycle of games.
GameServerInstance
The GameServerInstance class represents an instance of the game server that manages interactions with multiple clients (handlers) and processes messages related to the game state.
GameState
This enum class represents all the possible state of a game.
GameStateMessage
Message sent from server to client to communicate the current game state.
GetColorAvailableMessage
Message from client to server to request available colors for tokens.
GetGameListMessage
Message from client to server to request the list of available games.
GoldCard
The GoldCard class represents a gold card of the game.
GoldDeckMessage
Message sent from server to client to update the golden deck.
GoldFront
The GoldFront class represents the front face of a GoldCard.
GridMessage
Message sent from server to client to update the grid for a specific player.
GUIController
Abstract base class for GUI controllers.
GUIView
GUI representation of the client-side view.
HandCardsMessage
Message sent from server to client to update the hand cards of a player.
Handler
Defines the methods that must be implemented by any remote object acting as a handler for communication with clients via RMI.
HandObjectiveMessage
Message sent from server to client to update the objective cards in the player's hand.
HeartBeatMessage
Message from client to server to send a heartbeat signal.
InGameListener
Interface for listening to game status changes.
JoinGameMessage
Message from client to server to request joining an existing game.
Lobby
The Lobby class implements the Controller interface to manage lobby-related functionality and interactions.
LobbyGUIController
The controller of the scene where you have to choose between creating a new game or joining an old one
Message
Represents a generic message in the network communication.
NormalBack
The NormalBack class represents the back face of a ResourceCard or GoldCard.
NormalCard
The NormalCard class is an abstract class representing a playable card with a NormalBack back face.
NormalFace
The NormalFace class represents the front face of a ResourceCard or the back face of a StarterCard.
NotEnoughResourcesException
Exception thrown when there are not enough resources in the playground as required by the card.
NotStructuredObjectiveCard
Class NotStructuredObjectiveCard Description: This class represents an objective card that requires a non-structured collection of objects to be completed.
ObjectiveCard
Class ObjectiveCard Description: This abstract class represents an objective card in the game.
ObjectiveCardChosenMessage
Message from client to server indicating that a player has chosen an objective card.
PickMessage
Message from client to server indicating that a player has picked a displayed playable card.
PlayableCard
the PlayableCard is an abstract class representing all the cards that can be played on the playground, i.e.
PlayCardMessage
Message from client to server indicating that a player wants to play a card.
Player
Class Player Description: This class represents a player in the game.
PlayerColor
The PlayerColor enum represents the different colors that a player can choose in the game.
PlayerGround
The PlayerGround class represents the play area of a player in the game.
PlayersListMessage
Message sent from server to client to update the list of player nicknames.
PositionNotAvailableException
Exception thrown when a position is not available for a specific operation.
RecieveChatMessage
Message sent from server to client to deliver a chat message.
ResourceCard
The ResourceCard class represents a resource card of the game.
ResourceDeckMessage
Message sent from server to client to update the resource deck.
ResourcesMessage
Message sent from server to client to update the resources counter of a player.
RMIClient
This client communicates with the server using RMI.
RMIFactory
Defines the methods that must be implemented by any remote object acting as an RMI factory for creating and managing client-server communication handlers.
RMIFactoryImpl
The RMIFactoryImpl class implements the RMIFactory interface to provide RMI factory functionalities for creating and managing client-server communication handlers.
RMIHandlerClient
The RMIHandlerClient class handles the communication between the RMI client and the server.
RMIHandlerServer
The RMIHandlerServer class extends AbstractHandler and represents a server-side handler for managing RMI-based client-server communication.
ScoreMessage
Message sent from server to client to update the scores of players.
sendChatMessage
Message from client to server indicating a chat message to be sent.
SendGameListMessage
Message sent from server to client to provide the list of active games.
Server
The Server class manages communication between clients and game instances through both socket and RMI connections.
ServerApp
It starts the server application.
ServerToClientMessage
Represents a message sent from the server to the client.
SocketClient
The SocketClient class handles the communication between the client and the server using sockets.
SocketHandler
The SocketHandler class represents a handler for client connections via sockets in the server.
StarterCard
The StarterCard class represents a starter card of the game.
StarterFront
The StarterFront class represents the front face of a StarterCard This class is a subclass of Face and it adds the central Content list.
Structure
Enum Structure Description: This enum represents different possible structures required by some objective cards, the structured ones.
StructuredObjectiveCard
Class StructuredObjectiveCard Description: This class represents an objective card that requires a specific structure to be completed.
View
 
VirtualView
 
WaitingController
The controller of the scene where players wait for the game to start
WantThatColorMessage
Message from client to server indicating the chosen color.
WinnerMessage
Message sent from server to client to announce the winner of the game.
